mnemonics: Regular languages are a subset of Context-Free (CFL), which are a subset of Context-Sensitive (CSL), which are a subset of Recursively Enumerable (REL). Remember the order with 'Real Cats Frequently Sleep Relaxed'.
keyPoints: The Chomsky Hierarchy classifies formal grammars into four types (Type-0 to Type-3), each with an associated automaton and language family.
Type-3 (Regular) is the most restrictive, while Type-0 (Recursively Enumerable) is the most powerful.
Each level in the hierarchy is a proper subset of the level above it, meaning each class is strictly more powerful than the last.
commonMistakes: Assuming all Context-Free Languages are also Regular (they are not; e.g., {a^n b^n} is CF but not Regular).
Confusing Decidable problems (solvable by a Turing Machine that always halts) with Recognizable problems (solvable by a TM that may loop on 'no' answers).
